ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Badger
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells, +2 dice for forest spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: Badger shamans may go berserk in combat, the same as Bear shamans

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Bat
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection and manipulation spells, +1 die for spirits of the sky
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: +2 to all magical target numbers when in direct sunlight.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Boar
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells, +1 service from any spirit summoned for combat purposes
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells. Boar shamans must make a Willpower (6) Test to withdraw from conflict.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Bull
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health spells, +1 die for combat and detection spells
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: A Bull shaman must have a minimum Charisma of 4.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Cheetah
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells, +2 dice for savannah (prairie) spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for health spells. Cheetah shamans must have a minimum Reaction of 4.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Cobra
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice to combat and illusion spells, +1 die for jungle (forest) spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: If surprised (p. 108, SR3), a Cobra shaman adds a +1 modifier to all target numbers for the remainder of that combat.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Crab
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for sea spirits, +1 die for all Damage Resistance Tests (including Drain Tests)
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells. A Crab shaman must make a Willpower (6) Test to change his mind, much like Dog (p. 164, SR3).

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Crocodile
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells, +1 die for illusion spells, +2 dice for sea spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: Crocodile shamans may go berserk in the same way as Shark shamans (p. 166, SR3).

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Dove
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health spells, +1 die for detection spells, +1 die for spirits of the sky
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: Dove shamans cannot cast combat spells. They hate the thought of hurting others and must make a successful Willpower (6) Test to purposely inflict physical damage on a metahuman.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Elk
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +1 die for health spells, +1 die for spell defense, +2 dice for spirits of the land
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -2 dice for combat spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Fish
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection spells, +2 dice for one spirit of the waters (shaman's choice)
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Fox
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion spells, +2 dice for any one spirit of the land or spirit of man (shaman's choice)
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells. A Fox shaman must make a Willpower (6) Test to spare a fallen enemy.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Gecko
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion or manipulation spells (shaman's choice), +1 die for resisting any type of poison
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Goose
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection spells, +1 die for combat spells, +2 dice for a single spirit of the land, sky or waters (shaman's choice)
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: Goose shamans know their chosen territory well. Away from their home city or region, however, they suffer +2 to all magical target numbers. If the shaman moves to a new home, it takes a full turn of the moon (28 days) to acclimate to the new locale and eliminate the penalty.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Horse
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health spells, +2 dice for prairie spirits. Horse shaman initiates can also attempt to learn the Movement critter power (self only, three times a day; see p.265, SR3) as a metamagical technique.
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die when resisting combat or illusion spells (shaman must choose at character creation).

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Hyena
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells, +2 dice for Banishing any spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for health spells. Must make a Willpower (6) Test to perform an action with no benefit to herself.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Jackal
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection and illusion spells, +2 dice for savannah (prairie) spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for all combat spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Jaguar
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection spells, +2 dice for forest spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for health spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Leopard
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat and health spells, +2 dice for all nature spirits at night
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for resisting illusion spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Lizard
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health spells, +2 dice for desert, forest or mountain spirits (shaman's choice)
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: +2 to all target numbers while in tight quarters. When trapped in a confined place with no clear view of the sky, a Lizard shaman must make a Willpower (6) Test. The shaman flies into a berserk panic for 3 turns, minus 1 turn for every success generated on the Willpower Test. While in a panic, the shaman will do everything possible to get out of the enclosed space.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Monkey
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for manipulation spells, +2 dice for spirits of man
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Otter
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion spells, +2 dice for river or sea spirits (shaman's choice)
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Parrot
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice to illusion spells, +2 dice to jungle (forest) spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: Apply a +1 modifier to all magical target numbers when a Parrot shaman's magical actions are not witnessed by someone who can be impressed by them.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Polecat
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +1 die for combat spells (+1 more die for combat spells at night), +2 dice for spirits of the land
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for health spells. In combat, a Polecat shaman will single-mindedly continue to attack an opponent until they are downed, ignoring other opponents; the shaman must make a Willpower (6) Test to break off the attack.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Prairie Dog
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection spells, +1 die for illusion spells, +2 dice for spirits of the land
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -2 dice for combat spells. Prairie Dog shamans must have a minimum Charisma of 4.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Puma
ANIMAL TOTEMS.environment: Any isolated wilderness location except the desert
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion spells, +2 dice for mountain spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: +2 to all magical target numbers when in direct sunlight or in crowds

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Python
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health and control manipulation spells, +2 dice for jungle (forest) spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: A Python shaman must make a successful Willpower (6) Test to break off combat or any other sustained activity.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Scorpion
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat and illusion spells. Scorpion shamans can milk venom from ordinary and Awakened scorpions, and scorpion venom never does more than Light damage to a Scorpion shaman
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: +2 to all magical target numbers during the day, -1 die for all Conjuring Tests. Scorpion shamans become irritable and depressed when away from their desert homes: +1 to all of the shaman's magical target numbers for each day outside the desert to a maximum of +6.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Spider
ANIMAL TOTEMS.environment: The quiet, dark places into which others seldom look
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion spells, +1 die for all nature spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: +2 to all magical target numbers in the open, away from immediate shelter. +1 to all target numbers if a Spider shaman does not have sufficient time to plan and consider a situation.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Stag
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health and illusion spells, +2 dice for forest spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for manipulation spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Turtle
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion spells, +2 dice for one spirit of the waters (shaman's choice)
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -2 dice for combat spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Whale
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells, +2 dice for sea spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Bear
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health spells, +2 dice for forest spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: Bear shamans can go berserk when wounded. Whenever a Bear shaman takes physical damage in combat, the player makes a Willpower (4) Test. The shaman goes berserk for 3 turns, minus 1 turn per success. Three or more successes avert the berserk rage entirely. A berserk shaman will attack the closest living thing, friend or foe, using the most powerful weapons available (mundane or magical). If the shaman incapacitates a target before the time is up, the berserk fury dissipates.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Buffalo
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health spells, +2 dice for prairie spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Cat
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion spells, +2 dice for city spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: +1 to all Mental target numbers if dirty or unkempt. An unwounded Cat shaman must make a Willpower (6) Test when casting a damaging spell. If the test fails, the shaman must cast her least-damaging spell (at its minimum damage level, for a variable damage spell, and at 1/2 Force). If the shaman is wounded, all this playing around stops.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Coyote
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: None
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: None

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Dog
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection spells, +2 dice for field and hearth spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: A Dog shaman must make a Willpower (6) Test to change a declared course of action. The test requires a Complex Action as Dog struggles to change his mind.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Dolphin
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection spells, +2 dice for sea spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Eagle
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection spells, +2 dice for all spirits of the sky
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: Double the Essence loss caused by adding cyberware because of the psychological impact this has on the Eagle shaman.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Gator
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat and detection spells. As a wilderness totem +2 dice for swamp, lake or river spirits (shaman's choice). As an urban totem, +2 dice for city spirits.
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells. It takes a Willpower (6) Test for a Gator shaman to break off a fight, chase or other direct action.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Lion
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells, +2 dice for prairie spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for health spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Mouse
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection and health spells, +2 dice for hearth and field spirits.
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -2 dice for combat spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Owl
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for any Sorcery or Conjuring at night
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: +2 to all magical target numbers during the daytime.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Raccoon
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for manipulation spells, +2 dice for city spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Rat
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection and illusion spells, +2 dice for city spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Raven
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for manipulation spells, +2 dice for sky spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: +1 to all magical target numbers while not under the open sky

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Shark
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat and detection spells, +2 dice for sea spirits
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: Shark shamans can go berserk in combat similar to Bear shamans, when they are wounded or when they kill an opponent (see p. 163). A berserk Shark shaman may, instead of attacking a living target, continue to attack the body of his last victim, if the player chooses.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Snake
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection, health and illusion spells. As a wilderness totem, +2 dice for any one spirit of the land (shaman's choice). As an urban totem, +2 dice for any one spirit of man (shaman's choice).
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for all spells cast during combat.

ANIMAL TOTEMS.name: Wolf
ANIMAL TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat and detection spells, +2 dice for forest, prairie or mountain spirits (shaman's choice)
ANIMAL TOTEMS.disadvantages: Wolf shamans can go berserk in combat, similar to Bear shamans (p. 163).

TOTEMS.name: Badger
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells, +2 dice for forest spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Badger shamans may go berserk in combat, the same as Bear shamans

TOTEMS.name: Bat
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection and manipulation spells, +1 die for spirits of the sky
TOTEMS.disadvantages: +2 to all magical target numbers when in direct sunlight.

TOTEMS.name: Boar
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells, +1 service from any spirit summoned for combat purposes
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells. Boar shamans must make a Willpower (6) Test to withdraw from conflict.

TOTEMS.name: Bull
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health spells, +1 die for combat and detection spells
TOTEMS.disadvantages: A Bull shaman must have a minimum Charisma of 4.

TOTEMS.name: Cheetah
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells, +2 dice for savannah (prairie) spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for health spells. Cheetah shamans must have a minimum Reaction of 4.

TOTEMS.disadvantages: If surprised (p. 108, SR3), a Cobra shaman adds a +1 modifier to all target numbers for the remainder of that combat.

TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells. A Crab shaman must make a Willpower (6) Test to change his mind, much like Dog (p. 164, SR3).

TOTEMS.disadvantages: Crocodile shamans may go berserk in the same way as Shark shamans (p. 166, SR3).

TOTEMS.disadvantages: Dove shamans cannot cast combat spells. They hate the thought of hurting others and must make a successful Willpower (6) Test to purposely inflict physical damage on a metahuman.

TOTEMS.name: Elk
TOTEMS.advantages: +1 die for health spells, +1 die for spell defense, +2 dice for spirits of the land
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -2 dice for combat spells

TOTEMS.name: Fish
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection spells, +2 dice for one spirit of the waters (shaman's choice)
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells

TOTEMS.name: Fox
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion spells, +2 dice for any one spirit of the land or spirit of man (shaman's choice)
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells. A Fox shaman must make a Willpower (6) Test to spare a fallen enemy.

TOTEMS.name: Gecko
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion or manipulation spells (shaman's choice), +1 die for resisting any type of poison
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells

TOTEMS.name: Goose
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection spells, +1 die for combat spells, +2 dice for a single spirit of the land, sky or waters (shaman's choice)
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Goose shamans know their chosen territory well. Away from their home city or region, however, they suffer +2 to all magical target numbers. If the shaman moves to a new home, it takes a full turn of the moon (28 days) to acclimate to the new locale and eliminate the penalty.

TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die when resisting combat or illusion spells (shaman must choose at character creation).

TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for all combat spells

TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for resisting illusion spells

The shaman goes berserk for 3 turns, minus 1 turn per success. Three or more successes avert the berserk rage entirely. A berserk shaman will attack the closest living thing, friend or foe, using the most powerful weapons available (mundane or magical). If the shaman incapacitates a target before the time is up, the berserk fury dissipates.

TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health spells, +2 dice for prairie spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells

TOTEMS.advantages: None
TOTEMS.disadvantages: None

TOTEMS.name: Snake
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection, health and illusion spells. As a wilderness totem, +2 dice for any one spirit of the land (shaman's choice). As an urban totem, +2 dice for any one spirit of man (shaman's choice).
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for all spells cast during combat.

TOTEMS.name: Wolf
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat and detection spells, +2 dice for forest, prairie or mountain spirits (shaman's choice)
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Wolf shamans can go berserk in combat, similar to Bear shamans (p. 163).

TOTEMS.name: Moon
TOTEMS.environment: Wild places far from civilization, or the hidden corners of the city
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion and transformation manipulation spells, +1 die for detection spells, +1 die for spirits of the waters
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells. Moon shamans must make a Willpower (6) Test in order to engage in direct confrontation. Negotiation is not considered confrontation (Moon loves discussion), but arguments do fall into that category.

TOTEMS.name: Mountain
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for manipulation spells, +2 dice for mountain spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells. A Mountain shaman must make a Willpower (6) Test to change a course of action once it is chosen.

TOTEMS.name: Oak
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health spells, +2 dice for forest spirits, +2 dice for hearth spirits in any structure built mostly of oak
TOTEMS.disadvantages: An Oak shaman must have a minimum Strength and Body of 4.

TOTEMS.name: Sea
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health and transformation manipulation spells, +2 dice for sea spirits and ship (hearth) spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: A Sea shaman does not give anything away for free. She must receive a suitable payment, which is determined by the shaman. A loved one may receive a valuable artifact for the price of a kiss, while someone in disfavor must pay handsomely for the smallest consideration. A Sea shaman is very proud and must make a Willpower (6) Test to avoid answering any slight or insult in kind.

TOTEMS.name: Stream
TOTEMS.environment: Near the shores of a river or stream
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health spells, +2 dice for river spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells

TOTEMS.name: Sun
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat, detection and health spells. +2 dice for any spirit while in direct sunlight
TOTEMS.disadvantages: +2 to all Conjuring target numbers at night. A Sun shaman must have a minimum Charisma of 4.

TOTEMS.name: Wind
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection spells, +2 dice for spirits of the sky
TOTEMS.disadvantages: +2 to all magical target numbers while not under the open sky

TOTEMS.name: Fenrir
TOTEMS.advantages: +3 dice for combat spells, +1 die for forest spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Fenrir shamans must make a Willpower (8) Test to back down or flee from any confrontation. If wounded, a Fenrir shaman goes berserk in the same manner as a Bear shaman (p. 163, SR3).

TOTEMS.name: Gargoyle
TOTEMS.advantages: +1 die for detection and illusion spells, +2 dice for city spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for spirits of the waters. Gargoyle shamans must live in either a skyscraper or castlelike structure, which usually requires a high or luxury lifestyle (p. 240, SR3). Their homes always feature some sort of accessible perch, walkway or balcony near the roof.

TOTEMS.name: Griffin
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells, +2 dice for spirits of the sky
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Any time a Griffin shaman is insulted or offended, unless he succeeds in a Willpower (6) Test, the shaman will fly into a frenzy and attack the target.

TOTEMS.name: Leviathan
TOTEMS.advantages: +1 die for health and manipulation spells, +2 dice for sea spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells

TOTEMS.name: Pegasus
TOTEMS.environment: Rural area under the open sky
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice to detection and health spells, +2 dice for spirits of the sky
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Pegasus shamans cannot bear captivity or limits on their freedom of movement. If they voluntarily enter a building or enclosed area, they must make a Willpower (6) Test to remain inside. If they fail, they still may stay inside but receive +1 to all target numbers while inside. Every (Willpower) hours the shaman must repeat this test. Additional failures add a cumulative +1 modifier to all tests. If the modifiers reach +8, the shaman enters a death frenzy.

TOTEMS.name: Phoenix
TOTEMS.advantages: All Phoenix shamans summon spirits of the flames (MitS p.105) instead of spirits of man. +1 die for health and illusion spells, +2 dice for spirits of the flames. Phoenix shamans have a reputation, like their totem, for rising from the ashes. They can survive physical overflow damage of Body x 2. Each time the shaman's damage overflows the Physical column of the Condition Monitor, however, reduce the total by 1.
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Phoenix shamans cannot summon spirits of man. They must have a minimum Charisma of 4. Phoenix shamans must also know a performance skill such as a musical instrument, singing or dance, which may be used as a geas or as a creative skill used for centering (MitS p.72).

TOTEMS.name: Plumed Serpent
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection spells, +2 dice for spirits of the sky
TOTEMS.disadvantages: +2 to all magical target numbers outside the territorial borders of Aztlan

TOTEMS.name: Thunderbird
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat and detection spells, +2 dice for storm spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for all magical tests while not under open sky. Thunderbird shamans are very moody and subject to bouts of savage fury in the same manner as Shark shamans (p. 166, SR3).

TOTEMS.name: Unicorn
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health and illusion spells, +2 dice for spirits of the land. A Unicorn shaman receives the Aura Reading Skill (p. 86, SR3) for free at one-half his starting Intelligence. The shaman may increase the skill rating using standard rules.
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Double all Essence losses from cyberware.

TOTEMS.name: Wyrm
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health and manipulation spells, +2 dice for mountain spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Wyrm shamans must make a Willpower (6) Test to quit a task and do something else instead. They must also sleep an average of seventy hours a week.

TOTEMS.name: Adversary
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat and manipulation spells
TOTEMS.disadvantages: If wounded, Adversary shamans go berserk in the same way as Bear shamans (p. 163, SR3). Adversary shamans must succeed in a Willpower (8) Test to be friendly and civil to authority figures.

TOTEMS.name: Bacchus
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion spells, +2 dice for spirits of man
TOTEMS.disadvantages: A Bacchus shaman must succeed in a Willpower (6) Test to continue on a course of action if something more interesting, prettier or more relaxing presents itself. Additionally, Bacchus shamans are easily distracted; apply a -1 Perception die modifier whenever a Bacchus shaman is in the presence of music, art, motion or great beauty.

TOTEMS.name: Creator
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for enchanting, +1 die for hearth and city spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells. When confronted with something unusual or unique, a Creator shaman must make a Willpower (4) Test to avoid immediately using astral perception to examine the new find for 3 turns. Each success on the Willpower Test reduces this time by one turn, and 3 or more successes allow the shaman to ignore the impulse.

TOTEMS.name: Dark King
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health spells, +2 dice for spirits of man
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Followers of the Dark King are physically weak, having suffered great trials. They must sacrifice 1 point from a starting physical Attribute.

TOTEMS.name: Dragonslayer
TOTEMS.advantages: +3 dice for combat spells, +1 die for hearth spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion and detection spells

TOTEMS.name: Fire-Bringer
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection and manipulation spells, +2 dice for spirits of man
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells

TOTEMS.name: Great Mother
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for health spells, +2 dice for field and forest spirits and all spirits of the waters
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -2 dice when in the presence of corruption

TOTEMS.name: Horned Man
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells, +2 dice for all spirits of the land
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Shamans of the Horned Man must make a Willpower (6) Test to refuse a fight or physical contest. They are also lecherous and must make a Willpower Test against a target number equal to twice a seducer's Charisma to refuse any advances.

TOTEMS.name: Lover
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion and control manipulation spells, +2 dice for spirits of the waters
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Followers must have a minimum Charisma of 6.

TOTEMS.name: Moon Maiden
TOTEMS.advantages: None
TOTEMS.disadvantages: None

TOTEMS.name: Sea King
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for manipulation spells, +2 dice for sea spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells. Sea King shamans also suffer from the Sea Legs Flaw (p. 24, SR Comp), without receiving any compensating Edges or Build Points.

TOTEMS.name: Seductress
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion and control manipulation spells, +2 dice for spirits of man
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Seductress shamans must have a minimum Charisma of 6. They must also succeed in a Willpower (6) Test to avoid indulging themselves when a vice or corruption (drugs, BTLs, sex, and so on) is made available.

TOTEMS.name: Siren
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion and control manipulation spells, +2 dice for sea spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Siren shamans must have a minimum Charisma of 6. Sirens have difficulty focusing on more than one opponent at a time; they receive a +1 spellcasting modifier when attacked by more than one foe.

TOTEMS.name: Sky Father
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection and manipulation spells, +2 dice for storm spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: +2 to all target numbers if the shaman is entrapped or bound in any way

TOTEMS.name: Trickster
TOTEMS.advantages: None
TOTEMS.disadvantages: None

TOTEMS.name: Wild Huntsman
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for detection and illusion spells, +2 dice for storm spirits
TOTEMS.disadvantages: Wild Huntsman shamans can go berserk in combat in the same way as Bear shamans (p. 163, SR3).

TOTEMS.name: Wise Warrior
TOTEMS.advantages: +2 dice for combat and detection spells, +2 dice for resisting all damaging spells
TOTEMS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells

LOA.name: Azaca
LOA.disadvantages: A houngan of Azaca must make a Willpower (6) Test to avoid taking impulsive actions.

LOA.name: Damballah
LOA.advantages: +2 dice for detection and manipulation spells
LOA.disadvantages: A houngan of Damballah must make a successful Willpower (6) Test to reveal a particularly good piece of information.

LOA.name: Erzulie
LOA.advantages: +2 dice for illusion and control manipulation spells
LOA.disadvantages: A houngan of Erzulie must maintain at least a Middle Lifestyle (p. 240, SR3). They suffer +1 to all magical target numbers while unkempt or less than stylish.

LOA.name: Ghede
LOA.advantages: +2 dice for health and manipulation spells
LOA.disadvantages: A houngan of Ghede must make a Willpower (6) Test to avoid playing a trick in an inappropriate situation.

LOA.name: Legba
LOA.advantages: +2 dice for detection and manipulation spells
LOA.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells. Houngans of Legba must have a minimum Charisma of 4.

LOA.name: Obatala
LOA.advantages: +2 dice for detection, health and control manipulation spells
LOA.disadvantages: Houngans of Obatala cannot cast combat spells. They suffer +2 to all magical target numbers when they are not wearing at least one article of white clothing.

LOA.name: Ogoun
LOA.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells. Houngans of Ogoun must make a Willpower (6) Test to back down from any insult to their honor or prowess.

LOA.name: Shango
LOA.advantages: +3 dice for Fire and Lightning elemental manipulation spells
LOA.disadvantages: Houngans of Shango may go berserk in combat, in the same way as Bear shamans (p. 163, SR3).

ELEMENTS.name: Earth
ELEMENTS.advantages: +2 dice for manipulation spells and earth elementals
ELEMENTS.disadvantages: -1 die for detection spells and air elementals

ELEMENTS.name: Air
ELEMENTS.advantages: +2 dice for detection spells and air elementals
ELEMENTS.disadvantages: -1 die for manipulation spells and earth elementals

ELEMENTS.name: Fire
ELEMENTS.advantages: +2 dice for combat spells and fire elementals
ELEMENTS.disadvantages: -1 die for illusion spells and water elementals

ELEMENTS.name: Water
ELEMENTS.advantages: +2 dice for illusion spells and water elementals
ELEMENTS.disadvantages: -1 die for combat spells and fire elementals
